card.intuition: A specialized browser API that tells the browser you wish to perform an animation and requests that the browser calls a specified function to update an animation before the next repaint.
card.analogy: A film director waiting for the next 'frame' of the movie: instead of just throwing paint at the wall whenever you want (setTimeout), you wait for the camera operator to say 'Ready!' (the screen is ready to refresh) before you move the subject.
when_to_use: Smooth, high-performance visual animations (e.g., parallax, drag-and-drop)
Synchronizing JavaScript logic with the monitor's refresh rate (usually 60Hz or 144Hz)
gotchas: rAF is paused automatically by the browser when the tab is hidden or minimized, saving battery and CPU
Unlike `setInterval`, rAF doesn't guarantee a specific time; it fires as fast as the user's monitor can handle

steps.label: Schedule
steps.annotation: requestAnimationFrame schedules callback before next browser paint.

steps.label: Vsync
steps.annotation: Browser waits for vsync — typically 16.67ms frame budget.

steps.label: Callback fires
steps.annotation: rAF callback runs — reads layout, applies DOM changes.

steps.label: Paint
steps.annotation: Browser paints the frame. Smooth animation at display refresh rate.

steps.label: Next frame
steps.annotation: For continuous animation, call rAF again inside the callback.

exercise.prompt: Complete the recursive loop for a standard JS animation.
hints: The function name matches the concept title
